title: Attributes, Filters, Merge Drivers, and Content Policy · Independent Repository Trial
summary: An independent maintainer receives a different graph, repository policy, and failure constraint and must transfer attributes, filters, merge drivers, and content policy into a new path-attribute and content-transformation policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of path-attribute and content-transformation policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Resolve attributes from system, repository, info, directory, pattern, and negative states using check-attr evidence.
Design text, eol, working-tree-encoding, binary, and renormalization policy with byte-level and cross-platform verification.
Configure diff drivers, textconv, binary markers, merge drivers, recursive behavior, and conflict-marker size without treating presentation as storage.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — Case G73 is a lab in a disposable repository. a directory-level attribute overrides expected line-ending and filter policy. The learner must predict the effective text, eol, filter, diff, and merge states are reported for the exact path, run git check-attr --all -- scripts/release.sh, explain release.sh is text with eol=crlf because a nearer attribute rule overrides root policy, test this changed condition: remove the required filter tool and require checkout to fail instead of silently losing data, and preserve this recovery boundary: record byte digests before renormalization and retain the pre-migration ref.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for resolve attributes from system, repository, info, directory, pattern, and negative states using check-attr evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-large-content-policy-kk10l8" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Large Content Policy into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-large-content-policy-kk10l8
# scenario-key: trace-kk10l8 probe-8l01kk boundary-10l8kk owner-kk10l8x fault-kk10l8z
# before-ref: main=a11cekk candidate=b00b1l8
# hypothesis: a directory-level attribute overrides expected line-ending and filter policy
git check-attr --all -- scripts/release.sh
# expected: the effective text, eol, filter, diff, and merge states are reported for the exact path
# observation: release.sh is text with eol=crlf because a nearer attribute rule overrides root policy
# changed-case: remove the required filter tool and require checkout to fail instead of silently losing data
# after-ref: main=a11cekk candidate=c0ffee8
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: record byte digests before renormalization and retain the pre-migration ref
# transfer-limit: test real filters and encodings across supported platforms in disposable clones
